perturbation.original_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION. The obligations of this Agreement shall be continuing until the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient is no longer confidential
perturbation.changed_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION. The obligations of this Agreement shall be continuing until the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient is no longer confidential, or for a period of one year, whichever comes first.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. The original text implies an indefinite term as long as the information remains confidential. The added clause limits the agreement to one year, even if the information remains confidential. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality obligations. This contradicts Section 2 where there are no time contraints.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. All information disclosed by or on behalf of a party (“Discloser”) and/or its Affiliates during the term of this Agreement which is received, learned, or observed by the other party (“Recipient”) and/or its Affiliates is “Confidential Information,” including, but not limited to, business and financial information, marketing and strategic plans, customer lists, employee lists, reports, technologies, proc esses and operations, compilations, analyses, forecasts, studies, summaries, notes, data, product designs, drawings, prints, specifications, production schedules, test data, operations, inventions, know -how, component and part designs, schematics, formulat ions, samples, passwords, machines, network IDs, prototypes, mock -ups, templates, processing and control information, product performance data, manuals, purchase and sales records, warranty and repair records, marketing information, quotes and pricing info rmation, computer programs, or the like, including e- mail and electronic communication discussing any of the foregoing. Notwithstanding the forgoing, Confidential Information shall not include any information that is: (a) already lawfully in the possession of or known to Recipient as of the Effective Date without any obligation of confidentiality; (b) already in the public domain at the time of disclosure to Recipient or which, after such disclosure, enters into the public domain through no fault of Recipient; (c) lawfully furnished or disclosed to Recipient by a non- party to this Agreement without any obligation of confidentiality and through no wrongful act of Recipient; (d) independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Informati on; or (e) explicitly approved for public release by Discloser. GISF- 032 A - Non-Disclosure Agreement.doc Page 1 of 4 The foregoing list of exceptions is exhaustive and shall be strictly construed. In cases of doubt as to the use of Confidential Information, Recipient shall request that Discloser provide prior express written authorization of such use.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the obligations continue as long as the confidential information remains confidential, implying an indefinite term. The changed text limits this to one year, even if the information remains confidential. This directly contradicts Section 2, which defines 'Confidential Information' and sets obligations without any time constraints, leading to a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Confidential Information contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and may or may not be returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Confidential Information contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information. By changing 'shall be promptly returned' to 'may or may not be returned' introduces uncertainty. This alteration directly contradicts the enforceable nature of the return of materials, and the duty to preserve the confidenital information outlined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates return or destruction of confidential information. The changed text replaces 'shall be promptly returned' with 'may or may not be returned'. Section 5 requires Recipient to 'take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure'. Making the return or destruction of confidential information optional directly contradicts the enforceable nature of Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: 3. NON -DISCLOSURE. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person or entity. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any of its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates, except as required to carry out the Purpose under this Agreement for the benefit of Deere and its Affiliates. “Representative” shall mean any officer, director, employee, contractor, agent, supplier, or advisor of its respective entity. “Affiliate” shall mean any corporation, partnership, or other entity that, either directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party, where control is defined as having more than a fifty percent (50%) controlling inter est.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. NON -DISCLOSURE. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any other person or entity. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any of its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates, except as required to carry out the Purpose under this Agreement for the benefit of Deere and its Affiliates. “Representative” shall mean any officer, director, employee, contractor, agent, supplier, or advisor of its respective entity. “Affiliate” shall mean any corporation, partnership, or other entity that, either directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party, where control is defined as having more than a fifty percent (50%) controlling inter est.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of a Non-Disclosure Agreement. The original text strictly prohibits disclosure. Changing 'shall not disclose' to 'may disclose' grants Recipient the explicit permission to disclose confidential information at will. This renders the entire agreement meaningless and contradicts Section 5 where the Recipient is to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recipient 'shall not disclose Confidential Information'. The changed text alters this to 'may disclose Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts Section 5, which states 'Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure'. Allowing the recipient to disclose the information at will directly conflicts with their obligation to protect it.

perturbation.original_text: 13. REMEDIES. It is agreed that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement. Discloser is entitled to seek injunctive relief without proof of actual damages. In addition, Discloser may seek other appropriate relief, including monetary damag es. Unless stated otherwise, all remedies provided for in this Agreement are to be cumulative and in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other remedies available to either party at law, in equity or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. REMEDIES. It is agreed that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for breach of this Agreement. Discloser is not entitled to seek injunctive relief. In addition, Discloser may seek other appropriate relief, including monetary damag es. Unless stated otherwise, all remedies provided for in this Agreement are to be cumulative and in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other remedies available to either party at law, in equity or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: This removes Discloser's right to seek injunctive relief. In the original text it states that the Discloser is entitled to seek injunctive relief. This contradicts any possibility to protect the confidential information under Section 5 and creates uncertainty about the Discloser's means of protecting the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY. Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information. Prior to disclosure of Confidential Information to Recipient’s contractor, agent, supplier or advisor, Recipient shall ensure that such contractor, agent, supplier or advisor has executed a non -disclosure a greement for the benefit of Green Industrial Supply, Inc. in form and substance equivalent to this Agreement. Recipient shall be responsible for any misuse or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information by its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates. A disclosure of Confidential Information required under legal process shall not be considered to be a breach of confidentiality by Recipient or a waiver of such obligation by Discloser. Before any such disclosure, however, Recipient shall provide prompt written notice to Discloser to enable it to seek a protective order or prevent disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 13 states that "Discloser is entitled to seek injunctive relief", while the changed text removes this right. Section 5, regarding confidentiality, states that the "Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information...", implying that the Discloser has recourse to protect its confidential information. By removing the Discloser's right to seek injunctive relief, the changed text directly contradicts the intent and implied ability to enforce the confidentiality terms in Section 5, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. The parties agree not to assign any rights or delegate any duties under this Agreement without the other party’s prior written consent, and any attempt to do so is void and has no effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS. This Agreement will be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns. The parties agree they can freely assign any rights or delegate any duties under this Agreement without the other party’s prior written consent, and any attempt to do so is not void and has effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause regarding assignment. The original text prohibits assignment without prior written consent. The changed text permits assignment freely without consent. This could lead to disputes over who is bound by the agreement and who has rights under it. This changes the obligations specified in Section 3 where a breach in disclosure by a asignee will have the same effect as doing it by the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. NON -DISCLOSURE. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person or entity. Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any of its Representatives or Representatives of its Affiliates, except as required to carry out the Purpose under this Agreement for the benefit of Deere and its Affiliates. “Representative” shall mean any officer, director, employee, contractor, agent, supplier, or advisor of its respective entity. “Affiliate” shall mean any corporation, partnership, or other entity that, either directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party, where control is defined as having more than a fifty percent (50%) controlling inter est.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10 directly contradicts the original clause regarding assignment. The original text prohibits assignment without prior written consent, while the changed text permits assignment freely without consent. This directly impacts Section 3, which holds the Recipient responsible for non-disclosure. If the Recipient freely assigns their duties, as allowed in the Changed Text, and the assignee breaches the non-disclosure agreement, it directly contradicts the intent and enforceability of Section 3, which originally bound only the Recipient. The original and changed text creates a conflict making the two sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 12. SEVERABILITY AND WAIVER. If any provision of this Agreement is determined to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the rem aining provisions of this Agreement remain in full force and effect, so long as the essential terms and conditions of this Agreement reflect the original intent of the parties and remain valid, legal, and enforceable. The failure of a party to enforce a provision, exercise a right or pursue a default of this Agreement shall not be considered a waiver. The express waiver of a provision is to be effective only in the specific instance, and as to the specific purpose, for which it was given.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. SEVERABILITY AND WAIVER. If any provision of this Agreement is determined to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, all remaining provisions of this Agreement are also invalid, illegal or unenforceable. The failure of a party to enforce a provision, exercise a right or pursue a default of this Agreement shall be considered a waiver. The express waiver of a provision is to be effective only generally, and as to every purpose, for which it was given.
perturbation.explanation: By making if one provision of the Agreement illegal or unenforceable, the entire agreement gets thrown out contradicts the intent of the original clause. The original clause preserves the rest of the agreement, while the modified clause nullifies it. Also, by stating that The failure of a party to enforce a provision, exercise a right or pursue a default of this Agreement shall be considered a waiver makes the Recipient not responsible for keeping the materials and information as confidential and destroy or give them back.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement. Except as noted in the immediately following sentence, Confidential Information contained in electronically archived notes, reports, correspondences, emails, and other business records prepared by Recipient in connection with the Purpose under this Agreement need not be returned or destroyed, but shall be subject to all other terms of this Agreement. Confidential Information in the form of schematics, prints, drawings, formulations, and descriptions of processes and know -how, and the like, must be returned to Discloser or destroyed pursuant to this Section 6.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 12 directly contradicts section 6 regarding the return of materials. Originally, only a failure to enforce a provision isn't considered a waiver, protecting the Discloser's right to have materials returned. The changed text states that the failure to enforce a provision shall be considered a waiver which contradicts Discloser's right to have materials returned, effectively waiving that right. Also, the change making the entire agreement unenforceable contradicts the original clause that preserves the rest of the agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
